title: Safe moving and handling — the safe-handling sequence
source: CC79 Moving and Handling Policy and Procedure
scopeNote: Shows the general safe-handling sequence that applies to any manual task. Hoist transfer mechanics are covered separately under the hoists topic.
lottieSuggestion: manual handling, back safety, care worker

steps.label: Check the care plan first
steps.caption: Read the person's moving and handling plan before you start.

steps.label: Clear the route
steps.caption: Make sure the path is clear and you have somewhere to set down.

steps.label: Tell the person, let them help
steps.caption: Explain the move and let the person help where they can.

steps.label: Stand close, feet shoulder-width
steps.caption: Stand close to the person and place your feet shoulder-width apart.

steps.label: Bend your knees, not your back
steps.caption: Bend your knees and keep your back in its natural upright position.

steps.label: Agree one signal to move
steps.caption: One person calls it — ready, move, stop — and you move together.

steps.label: Move smoothly, turn your feet
steps.caption: Keep the person close, and turn your feet instead of twisting your back.

endFrame: Two staff means two. Faulty equipment or changed needs? Stop and tell your manager.
